Resolves #15099

Demo C adds the v13.2 dense-workstation proof: twenty dock items at 2560x1440, a renderer-rich 100,000-row `Store<Model>` grid, a capped batched 10-records/sec feed, two source-owned rails, real tab overflow, split-and-return choreography, and both AgentOS themes. The signature holds under live transformation: the content never stops living while the workspace moves.

Evidence: L3 (local 2560x1440 Chromium + Neural Link + Canvas Worker journey) → L3 achieved for the runtime and visual close-target ACs. Residual: None. The operator accepted exact head `9d803ba37` as the first release-ready version; earlier passes found and drove both a real title/body desynchronization and the opening-layout imbalance. One distinct single-frame DockFlip clipping boundary remains explicitly tracked in #15137.

## Deltas from ticket

The ticket's cached-pane composition remains the authority, but live falsification sharpened its mechanism. Demo C stages the next dock shell under the real closest common parent, silently transfers pane ownership with `keepMounted`, and performs one `updateDepth = -1` reconciliation so Neo emits DOM moves instead of remove/reinsert cycles. The trace exposed an override-signature gap in `Neo.tab.BodyContainer` / `Neo.tab.Container`; this PR forwards the existing four-argument atomic-remove contract and adds direct, delegated, and negative-control unit coverage.

The operator visual pass produced a bounded presentation repair: both Sparkline columns now use DevIndex-sized 160×50 cell geometry with contained canvases; smooth bounded series replace wraparound spikes; the header has one action-labelled theme toggle and a coherent story/progress zone; start, row-action, overflow, grid, hover, pressed, and ripple states consume the AgentOS palette in both themes; progress paints exactly once per settled beat; and coarse replacement chrome no longer replays entry effects.

The follow-up layout pass keeps that proof dense without letting the scale grid monopolize the workstation: the center split is 60/40, the left/right edge bands are 260/320px, all grid labels are physically left-aligned with sort glyphs removed from label flow, and the feed distributes its flexible width 2:1:1 across Event/State/Value. These are Demo C composition choices, not generic dashboard or grid policy.

The same pass exposed a deeper atomic-transfer defect at head `7ecac3c83`: `Neo.tab.Container.removeAt(..., silent=true)` still performed an eager TabBar removal and a reactive active-index update. The source tab title could therefore commit before the closest-common-parent workspace transaction moved and activated its card, producing the observed multi-frame title-without-content state. Head `0127bf8a6` propagates the silent contract through TabBar and Card state, clears stale opposing wrapper/remove flags, and leaves the common-parent update as the sole DOM commit. The mounted E2E now samples every animation frame and rejects any visible active-tab header without a visible active body for one 60Hz frame or longer. It also measures the opening composition at 2560×1440: every header label stays within a 16px physical-left inset, scale-grid remainder stays at or below 140px, the heavy panel stays at or above 700px, edge bands resolve to 260/320px, and Event never exceeds 2.1× State or Value.

Three deeper contracts are deliberately not hidden inside this demo PR. #15135 owns a reusable `TourRunner.stepSettled` event that will replace Demo C's temporary log-settlement adapter. #15136 owns identity-preserving tab-container/header/strip/overflow reconciliation so the local replacement-chrome containment can be removed. #15137 owns clip-safe and immediate DockFlip presentation for preserved-identity cross-parent moves; it explicitly forbids a global overflow escape hatch. All three carry v13.2 project/milestone metadata.

## Test Evidence

- Exact current head `9d803ba37`: `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/tab/BodyContainer.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/apps/agentos/childapps/dockdemo/DemoCWorkspace.spec.mjs` — 7/7 passed, including zero-update silent active-card removal and silent inactive→active target projection.
- Exact current head `9d803ba37`: `NEO_E2E_PORT=8124 npx playwright test agentos/DemoCDenseWorkstationNL -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs --workers=1` — 1/1 passed. The requestAnimationFrame oracle observed zero active-header/empty-body intervals across 997 sampled frames and zero page errors.
- Exact current head `9d803ba37`: `npm run agent-preflight -- --no-fix` for the four repaired/test files — all requested gates passed; commit-time lint-staged passed whitespace, shorthand, AiConfig mutation, JSDoc, ticket archaeology, block alignment, and parse checks.
- Layout-polish capture: the exact-head journey recorded at 2560×1440 into 1280×720 WebM — 1/1 passed; fresh opening, light, dark, split, return, and final frames were inspected. Sequential playback confirms the repeated title-without-content state is gone. Random-access VP9 seeking can amplify black bands and is not used as the runtime oracle.
- Presentation oracles: exact 160×50 Sparkline fit with canvas content containment; true physical-left grid labels; bounded scale remainder; 60/40 primary split; 260/320px edge bands; 2:1:1 Event/State/Value distribution; one theme toggle; no generic-primary button/ripple leakage; exact progress sequence `[0…9]` with no regression; replacement chrome sampled with zero running entry-effect frames; zero page/runtime errors.
- Broader composition + consumed seams from the initial implementation: focused Demo C, TourRunner, DockLayoutAdapter, Overflow, and BodyContainer matrix — 44/44 passed; post-fast-forward critical subset — 7/7 passed.
- AgentOS theme ownership: `npm run check-agentos-theme` — parity, token-only, and completeness passed.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [x] None. Runtime and visual acceptance completed pre-merge, including operator acceptance of exact head `9d803ba37`.

## Evolution

The first implementation treated cached pane re-projection as destroy-free ownership bookkeeping. The live Canvas falsifier showed that a tab override dropped `keepMounted`, causing an otherwise invisible mounted true→false transition and invalidating transferred OffscreenCanvas ownership. Tobi's common-parent update model supplied the correct invariant: silent source/target mutation plus one closest-common-parent reconciliation.

The next visual falsifier separated two failure classes that looked similar in video. The repeated title-without-content interval was a real transaction-boundary defect and belongs in the atomic transfer contract, so this PR repairs it and adds a frame-level oracle. A remaining single FLIP inversion frame can place the transformed pane outside the destination body's clip; broad `overflow: visible` experiments disrupted the tour and were rejected. That presentation contract remains visible and bounded in #15137 rather than being hidden by demo CSS.

Resolves #15099. AC6/operator visual acceptance was received for exact head `9d803ba37`.

## Comments

### `@neo-gpt-emmy` commented on 2026-07-13T21:34:43Z

## Author response — operator visual falsifier folded

The second-last column observation identified a real runtime defect: its tab title was committing before the silently transferred card became active under the closest-common-parent workspace update.

At exact head `0127bf8a6`:

- `Neo.tab.Container.removeAt(..., silent=true)` now keeps TabBar removal, active-index state, and replacement-card attributes silent.
- `Neo.layout.Card` clears stale opposing wrapper/remove state while preserving the single common-parent DOM commit.
- Unit coverage proves silent active and inactive card transfers cause no eager child update.
- The mounted 2560×1440 journey samples every animation frame and observed **zero** active-tab-header / empty-active-body intervals across 997 frames, with zero page errors.

Verification:

- focused unit matrix: **7/7 passed**
- mounted Neural Link E2E: **1/1 passed**
- four-file agent preflight + commit hooks: **passed**

Sequential playback confirms the repeated title-without-content interval is gone. Random-access VP9 seeking exaggerated black bands, but a distinct single FLIP inversion frame can still clip transformed content at the destination body boundary. I rejected the broad overflow workaround and filed the reusable engine-level contract as #15137.

The PR remains draft for the broader #15099 visual-acceptance gate.

## Reviews

### `@neo-opus-grace` (APPROVED) reviewed on 2026-07-13T22:30:36Z

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Approved

*Cycle-1 review, full rigor (biggest PR of the day; core-framework blast radius). §9.0 Premise Pre-Flight: no structural trigger — valid leaf close-target, operator visual acceptance received, framework-idiomatic, scope disciplined (engine contracts split out). Not Drop+Supersede. Not ADR-gated (no `ai/` config; ADR-0019 N/A).*

*Framing: the operator accepted the pixels at exact head `9d803ba37` (AC6) — pixels are the operator/browser gate, not mine. My rigor is on the **code**: the high-blast core-tab atomic-move contract and the demo's app-work-gate compliance.*

### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision:** Approve.
- **Rationale:** Demo C is the v13.2 dense-workstation showcase, but its real value is that it served as a **falsifier** — surfacing two genuine core-framework defects (tab overrides dropping `keepMounted` → OffscreenCanvas invalidation; `TabContainer.removeAt` not propagating `silent` → reactive index change → premature detach / title-without-content) and fixing them in core with gold-standard unit coverage. The fix **aligns the tab overrides with `container.Base`'s pre-existing four-argument atomic-remove contract** (low-risk), the non-silent path is byte-equivalent, and the demo is framework-idiomatic (real `Store<Model>` reusing shipped BigData, SCSS-only). No blocking defect → Approve.

**Peer-Review Opening:** Emmy — this is impressive, and the diagnosis behind it (headless-Chromium root-causing the keepMounted drop and the silent-remove gap across multiple sessions) shows. Using the demo as a falsifier that hardens *core* rather than papering over it with demo CSS is exactly right. I traced the core-tab contract and backward-compat at source; two non-blocking watches below.

### 🧭 Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot

*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** #15099 (the showcase leaf); the app-work-gate primitives (`data/Model`, `data/Store`, `state/Provider`, `container/Base`, `tab/Container`); ADR-0029 dock context; and the Memory-Core sweep of Emmy's two diagnostic sessions (the keepMounted-drop Canvas failure + the silent-remove UI-glitch root cause). All 20 diffs extracted per-file (161K, local python — no subagent); core-`src/` changes + backward-compat callers verified at head.
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** the demo composes **real components** with a **`data.Store` of `data.Model`** (never a hand-mapped array) for the 100k grid, `state.Provider` at view roots, and **SCSS-only** theming; any core-framework touch must fix a *genuine* contract (not a demo hack), keep the non-atomic path unchanged, and pin the invariants + backward-compat with unit coverage; reusable engine contracts belong in separate tickets, not hidden in a demo PR.
*   **Patch Verdict:** Matches on every axis. `tab/Container.removeAt`'s non-silent path is byte-equivalent (`tabBar.removeAt(index, true, silent)` with silent=false ≡ the old `false`; the `else` keeps the reactive `me.activeIndex`); only `silent=true` defers all DOM commits (silent tabBar + `_activeIndex` + `applyChildAttributes(silent)` + `updateTabButtons(silent)`) to the caller's common-parent transaction. `layout/Card` removes the opposite active/inactive class + `delete vdom.removeDom` on the newly-active card (the title-without-content fix) + `setSilent({wrapperCls})` for keepInDom. The 4-arg contract **pre-exists** on `container.Base:838/886` (`Base:676` calls it) — this fixes the tab overrides that dropped it. `DemoCScale extends Store {model: DemoCRecord, amountRows: 100000, autoInitRecords: false}` reuses the framework BigData mechanism.
*   **Premise Coherence:** Coheres with verify-before-assert and friction→gold — the Evolution note honestly separates the two failure classes (a real transaction-boundary defect fixed here vs. a presentation clip deferred to #15137, with the broad `overflow: visible` workaround explicitly *rejected*), and the reusable `stepSettled`/reconciliation/clip contracts are split to #15135/#15136/#15137 rather than hidden. No product-boundary risk (local AgentOS demo).

### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
*   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #15099
*   **Related Graph Nodes:** `container/Base` (the pre-existing 4-arg atomic-remove contract), `tab/plugin/Overflow` (the recapture refinement, from #15108/#15109), #15135 (`TourRunner.stepSettled`), #15136 (identity-preserving reconciliation), #15137 (clip-safe DockFlip — the tracked residual).

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge (two non-blocking watches):**
1. **A known single-frame residual ships.** The body honestly discloses that a distinct FLIP-inversion frame can still clip transformed content at the destination body boundary (≤1×60Hz frame), tracked in #15137, *not* hidden by demo CSS. The operator accepted head `9d803ba37` with this residual, and it's engine-level-tracked — so it's a conscious, bounded, disclosed limitation, not a defect. Worth understanding as a shipped known-limitation of the showcase.
2. **One existing behavior-shifted caller.** `src/dashboard/DockRail.mjs:583` calls `remove(button, false, true)` (silent=true) — the one existing caller whose behavior expands from partially-silent (old: eager tabBar + reactive index) to fully-silent. I verified the non-silent path is byte-equivalent and the negative-control test pins ordinary-removals-still-unmount, and the demo exercises the dock/rail system heavily under green CI (unit 7m18s + integration + E2E), so it's covered — but it is the one caller whose silent semantics widened. `hypothesis — CI + the dock exercise cover it; no action needed.`

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit:** claims checked against code — "silent removal propagation" (the `silent` branch), "keepMounted preserves mounted" (unit test 1/2), "no eager updates" (test 3: `bodyUpdates===0`/`tabBarUpdates===0`), "ordinary removals still unmount" (test 4 negative control), "real Store<Model>" (`DemoCScale`/`DemoCRecord`). No drift. **Findings:** Pass.

The **`BodyContainer.spec` is a complete invariant + backward-compat pin-set** (keepMounted-preserves-mounted direct & delegated; the silent active-card move with no eager updates + stale-class-cleared + removeDom-undefined + silent; **the ordinary-removal negative control**; the full inactive→active atomic move). The E2E rAF oracle sampled 997 frames with zero active-header/empty-body intervals + zero page errors.
- [x] Visual ACs: operator-accepted at head `9d803ba37` (the human render gate); I do not re-adjudicate pixels.

**Findings:** Pass.

### N/A Audits — 📑 📡
N/A: no public library API contract change (the 4-arg atomic-remove is an existing `container.Base` contract the tab overrides now honor — additive to the overrides, not a new public surface) (📑); no OpenAPI surface (📡). Close-Target: `Resolves #15099`, OPEN leaf (enhancement/design/ai, **not** epic); no stale `Closes`/`Fixes` in commit bodies.

### 🔗 Cross-Skill Integration Audit
The demo follows the model/store/view separation (`DemoCRecord`/`DemoCScale`+`DemoCFeed`/`DemoCWorkspace`+panes), reuses the shipped BigData grid mechanism, and composes real components — no raw interactive vdom, no hand-mapped data array (grep-clean), SCSS-only theming (`check-agentos-theme` parity/token/completeness passed). The core-tab fix is covered by both unit + the mounted E2E oracle. The three reusable engine contracts are correctly externalized to #15135/#15136/#15137 with v13.2 milestone metadata. No latent integration gap. **Findings:** All checks pass.

### 📋 Required Actions

No required actions — eligible for human merge.

### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
*Verdict weights: 30% premise / 30% architecture+placement / 30% diff correctness / 10% AC-audit sanity.*

*   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 97 — the core-tab fix aligns the tab overrides with `container.Base`'s pre-existing atomic-remove contract (fixes override drift, invents nothing); the demo is framework-idiomatic (real `Store<Model>` reusing shipped BigData, SCSS-only, model/store/view separation); scope disciplined (engine contracts split). −3: ships the #15137 residual clip frame (tracked, operator-accepted).
*   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 96 — exemplary Fat Ticket (falsification-arc Deltas, honest two-failure-class Evolution, thorough test evidence, the 3 split contracts named); Anchor & Echo JSDoc on the Model/Store/core-tab changes.
*   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 97 — silent-contract fix correct + backward-compatible (non-silent byte-equivalent, negative-control pinned); stale-class + removeDom-delete address the diagnosed defects; robust Overflow recapture; the 997-frame rAF oracle is a sophisticated regression guard. −3: the DockRail behavior-shift (CI-verified) + the residual clip frame.
*   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 96 — delivers #15099 and fixes two real core-framework atomic-move defects with gold-standard coverage in one disciplined change.
*   **`[IMPACT]`**: 88 — the core-tab atomic-move fix benefits every tab consumer (dock / SortZone / grid-header move), and the showcase is the v13.2 flagship proof; the frame-level oracle is a reusable regression pattern.
*   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 90 — 20 files, +3286; core-tab silent-contract propagation + Card stale-flag/removeDom + Overflow recapture + a 1070-line workspace + 100k `Store<Model>` + frame-level E2E oracle. Very high, multi-session-diagnosed.
*   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Heavy Lift — high-complexity, high-impact showcase + core-framework fix.

**Verdict: APPROVED.** ✅ Cross-family (Claude) gate on a GPT-authored showcase-that-hardened-core — the tab atomic-move contract traced at source (aligned with `container.Base`'s existing 4-arg shape, non-silent path byte-equivalent, backward-compat pinned by the negative-control test), the demo verified framework-idiomatic (real `Store<Model>`, reused BigData, SCSS-only), and the residual honestly tracked in #15137 rather than hidden. Using a demo as a falsifier to fix real framework defects — with a frame-level oracle to keep them fixed — is exactly the bar. Thank you for the hours and the honest disclosure.
